title: Create picture graphs (picture more than 1)
description: Create and interpret picture graphs.  Each picture on the graph represents a number greater than 1.

assessment_items.question_content:
Enzo trains monkeys to do flips in the trapeze show at the circus.
 
Monkey  | Number of flips 
:- | :-: | -
Tumbler | $25$ 
Rolly | $35$ 
Topple | $20$ 
Twisty  | $40$  
 Gymbo | $15$  

**Create a picture graph to show the number of flips each monkey does in the trapeze show.**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints:
Each
[[☃ image 1]]
 equals $\blueD5$ flips.

Tumbler did $\redC{25}$ flips in the trapeze show.

$\blueD5 \times \greenD5 = \redC{25}$, so we put $\greenD5$ pictures above Tumbler.  

[[☃ image 1]]
Now, let's fill in the pictures for the other $4$ columns.

Here is the completed graph:

[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: A picture of a monkey equals 5 flips.

assessment_items.question_content:
Daniela keeps a rain barrel in her backyard so she can keep track of the amount of rain that fell each month.

Month | Millimeters of rain 
:- | :-: 
January | $45$ 
February | $18$ 
March | $63$ 
April  | $72$ 
May | $36$ 

**Create a picture graph to show the amount of rain that fell in Daniela's rain barrel each month.**

[[☃ image 1]]

 

[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints:
Each 

[[☃ image 1]]

 equals $\blueD{9}$ millimeters of rain.

$\maroonD{45}$ millimeters of rain fell in Daniela's rain barrel in January.

$\blueD{9} \times \greenD5 = \maroonD{45}$, so we put $\greenD5$ pictures above January.  

[[☃ image 1]]
Now, let's fill in the pictures for the other $4$ columns.

Here is the completed graph:


[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: A picture of a raindrop equals 9 millimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
Scarlett Squirrel teaches young squirrels how to hula dance. She records attendance at her dance class every day.

Day  | Number of squirrels 
:- | :-: 
Monday | $14$ 
Tuesday  | $10$ 
Wednesday | $8$ 
Thursday  | $10$  
Friday  | $12$ 

**Create a picture graph to show the number of squirrels at hula dance class each day this week.**


[[☃ image 1]]

 

[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints:
Each 

[[☃ image 1]]

 equals $\blueD2$ squirrels.

On Monday, $\maroonD{14}$ squirrels were at hula dance class.

$\blueD2 \times \greenD7 = \maroonD{14}$, so we put $\greenD7$ pictures above Monday.


[[☃ image 1]]
Now, let's fill in the pictures for the other $4$ columns.

Here is the completed graph:


[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: A picture of a squirrel equals 2 squirrels.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ms. Rossi keeps a chart of how many baby teeth her students have lost this year.  

Student | Number of teeth 
:- | :-: 
Ana | $8$ 
Murat | $6$ 
Manco | $2$ 
Sally  | $10$  
Isla  | $4$ 

**Create a picture graph to show the number of baby teeth each student has lost this year.**

[[☃ image 1]]

 

[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints:
Each

[[☃ image 1]]

 equals $\blueD{2}$ teeth.

Ana has lost $\maroonD{8}$ baby teeth this year.

$\blueD{2} \times \greenD4 = \maroonD{8}$, so we put $\greenD4$ pictures above Ana.  


[[☃ image 1]]
Now, let's fill in the pictures for the other $4$ columns.

Here is the completed graph:


[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: A picture of a tooth equals 2 teeth.

assessment_items.question_content:
Olivia loves I Spy books.  In her favorite one, she tries to see how many times she can find the sly fox on each page of the book.

Page | Number of fox sightings 
:- | :-: 
Page $1$ | $20$ 
Page $2$ | $8$ 
Page $3$ | $12$  
Page $4$  | $20$  
Page $5$ | $16$ 

**Create a picture graph to show the number of times Olivia finds the sly fox on each page.**


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints:
Each 

[[☃ image 1]]

equals $\blueD{4}$ fox sightings.

Olivia found the fox $\maroonD{20}$ times on page $1$.

$\blueD{4} \times \greenD5 = \maroonD{20}$, so we put $\greenD5$ pictures above Page $1$.  


[[☃ image 1]]
Now, let's fill in the pictures for the other $4$ columns.

Here is the completed graph:

[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: A picture of a fox equals 4 fox sightings.

assessment_items.question_content:
Leo and his friends are so excited that a new book in their pirate series came out yesterday.  They each bought a copy of the book and stayed up late reading. 

Person | Number of pages 
:- | :-: 
Leo | $70$ 
Ogechi | $80$
Antoine | $40$  
Budi  | $70$ 
Nicolas | $50$  

**Create a picture graph to show the number of pages each person has read in the new pirate book so far.**


[[☃ image 1]]

 

[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints:
Each

[[☃ image 1]]

 equals $\blueD{10}$ pages read.

Leo has read $\maroonD{70}$ pages so far.

$\blueD{10} \times \greenD7 = \maroonD{70}$, so we put $\greenD7$ pictures above Leo.  

[[☃ image 1]]
Now, let's fill in the pictures for the other $4$ columns.

Here is the completed graph:


[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: A picture of a book equals 10 pages read.

assessment_items.question_content:
Rose's flower shop makes bouquets for special occasions.  Each bouquet comes with a different number of flowers.

Bouquet  | Number of flowers 
:- | :-: 
Anniversary | $21$ 
Birthday | $18$ 
Congratulations  | $12$ 

**Create a picture graph to show the number of flowers in each bouquet.**


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints:
Each 

[[☃ image 1]]

equals $\blueD3$ flowers.

The Anniversary bouquet has $\maroonD{21}$ flowers.

$\blueD3 \times \greenD7 = \maroonD{21}$, so we put $\greenD7$ pictures above Anniversary.


[[☃ image 1]]
Now, let's fill in the pictures for the other $2$ columns.

Here is the completed graph:


[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: A picture of a flower equals 3 flowers.

assessment_items.question_content:
Zoo Town's baseball league names all the teams after cats. The league even calls baseball players "cats."

Team |Bobcats | Tigers | Panthers | Lions | Cheetahs
- |:-: | :-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
Players |$24$ | $24$ | $20$ | $16$ | $28$

**Create a picture graph to show the number of cats on each baseball team.**

[[☃ image 1]]

 

[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints:
Each 

[[☃ image 1]]

 equals $\blueD4$ cats.

There are $\maroonD{24}$ cats on the Bobcat baseball team.

$\blueD4 \times \greenD6 = \maroonD{24}$, so we put $\greenD6$ pictures above Bobcats.  


[[☃ image 1]]
Now, let's fill in the pictures for the other $4$ columns.

Here is the completed graph:


[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: A picture of a baseball equals 4 cats.

assessment_items.question_content:
The basketball player who scores the most points for the season wins a special recognition award at school.

Player |Amit | Dina| Carolina| Antonio
- |:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
Points |$32$ | $64$ | $40$ | $56$

**Create a picture graph to show the number of points each player scored this season.**


[[☃ image 1]]

 

[[☃ plotter 1]]
hints: Each basketball equals $\blueD8$ points.

Amit scored $\maroonD{32}$ points.

$\blueD8 \times \greenD4 = \maroonD{32}$, so we put $\greenD4$ pictures above Amit's name.


[[☃ image 1]]
Now, let's fill in the pictures for the other $3$ columns.

Here is the completed graph:

[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: A picture of a basketball equals 8 points.

assessment_items.question_content:
Nora's class is sending cards to children staying at the local children's hospital. Below is a picture graph that shows how many cards her class sent each month.



[[☃ image 1]]$=9\,\text{cards}$


[[☃ image 2]]

**Complete the chart using the picture graph.**

Month | Number of cards sent
- | :-: | -
September| [[☃ input-number 5]]
November | [[☃ input-number 6]]
January | [[☃ input-number 7]]
March | [[☃ input-number 8]]
May | [[☃ input-number 9]]
hints:
Each 
[[☃ image 1]]
 equals $\goldD{9}$ cards.

We need to count how many pictures are over each month on the picture graph.  

[[☃ image 1]]


There are $\blueD7$ pictures over September.

$\blueD7 \times \goldD{9} = \greenD{63}$

The class sent $63$ cards September.

Here is the completed chart:

Month | Number of cards sent
- | :-: | -
September| $7 \times \goldD{9} = \greenD{63}$
November| $8 \times \goldD{9} = \greenD{72}$
January| $6\times \goldD{9} = \greenD{54}$
March| $10 \times \goldD{9} = \greenD{90}$
May| $11 \times \goldD{9} = \greenD{99}$

options.alt: A picture of an envelope.

A picture graph shows the horizontal axis labeled Month and the vertical axis labeled Number of cards. Each month is listed along the horizontal axis from left to right as follows: September, November, January, March, and May. The number of cards in each month is represented by the number of pictures of envelopes plotted on the graph. September is represented by 7 pictures of envelopes, November is represented by 8 pictures of envelopes, January is represented by 6 pictures of envelopes, March is represented by 10 pictures of envelopes, and May is represented by 11 pictures of envelopes.

assessment_items.question_content:
Moe has $4$ mice as pets. He created a picture graph to show how much cheese each mouse eats. 


[[☃ image 1]]


[[☃ image 2]]

**Complete the chart using the picture graph.**

Mouse name | Grams of cheese eaten
- | :-: | -
Whiskers| [[☃ input-number 1]]
Tiny Toes| [[☃ input-number 2]]
Missy Mouse| [[☃ input-number 3]]
Beady| [[☃ input-number 4]]
hints:
Each 

[[☃ image 1]]

 equals $\goldD{8}$ grams of cheese.

We need to count how many pictures are over each mouse on the picture graph.  


[[☃ image 1]]

There are $\blueD4$ pictures over Whiskers.

$\blueD4 \times \goldD{8} = \greenD{32}$

Whiskers ate $32$ grams of cheese.

Here is the completed chart:

Mouse name | Grams of cheese eaten
- | :-: | -
Whiskers| $4 \times \goldD{8} = \greenD{32}$
Tiny Toes| $6 \times \goldD{8} = \greenD{48}$
Missy Mouse| $3\times \goldD{8} = \greenD{24}$
Beady| $3 \times \goldD{8} = \greenD{24}$

options.alt: A picture of cheese equals 8 grams.

A picture graph shows the horizontal axis labeled Mouse name and the vertical axis labeled Grams of cheese eaten. Each Mouse name is listed along the horizontal axis from left to right as follows: Whiskers, Tiny Toes, Missy Mouse, and Beady. The grams of cheese eaten by each mouse is represented by the number of pictures of cheese plotted on the graph. Whiskers is represented by 4 pictures of cheese, Tiny Toes is represented by 6 pictures of cheese, Missy Mouse is represented by 3 pictures of cheese, and Beady is represented by 3 pictures of cheese.

assessment_items.question_content:
Pedro sells hot dogs at the baseball stadium. He created a picture graph to show how many hot dogs he sold in the first five innings of today's game.


[[☃ image 1]]


[[☃ image 2]]

**Complete the chart using the picture graph.**

Inning | Number of hot dogs sold
- | :-: | -
First| [[☃ input-number 1]]
Second | [[☃ input-number 2]]
Third| [[☃ input-number 3]]
Fourth| [[☃ input-number 4]]
Fifth| [[☃ input-number 5]]
hints:
Each 

[[☃ image 1]]

 equals $\goldD{10}$ hot dogs.

We need to count how many pictures are over each inning on the picture graph.  


[[☃ image 1]]

There are $\blueD7$ pictures over First.

$\blueD7 \times \goldD{10} = \greenD{70}$

$70$ hot dogs were sold in the first inning.

Here is the completed chart:

Inning |  Number of hot dogs sold
- | :-: | :-:
First|$7 \times \goldD{10} = \greenD{70}$
Second|$4\times \goldD{10} =  \greenD{40}$
Third|$6 \times \goldD{10} =  \greenD{60}$
Fourth|$4 \times \goldD{10} = \greenD{40}$
Fifth|$2 \times \goldD{10} = \greenD{20}$

options.alt: A picture of a hot dog equals 10 hot dogs.

A picture graph shows the horizontal axis labeled Inning and the vertical axis labeled Number of hot dogs sold. Each Inning is listed along the horizontal axis from left to right as follows: First, Second, Third, Fourth, and Fifth. The number of hot dogs sold is represented by the number of pictures of hot dogs plotted on the graph. First is represented by 7 pictures of hot dogs, Second is represented by 4 pictures of hot dogs, Third is represented by 6 pictures of hot dogs, Fourth is represented by 4 pictures of hot dogs, and Fifth is represented by 2 pictures of hot dogs.

assessment_items.question_content:
Otter Experience Boat Tours created a picture graph to show how many otters were seen on their tours this week.


[[☃ image 1]]


[[☃ image 2]]

**Complete the chart using the picture graph.**

Day | Number of otters seen 
- | :-: | -
Friday | [[☃ input-number 2]]
Saturday| [[☃ input-number 3]]
Sunday | [[☃ input-number 4]]
hints:
Each 

[[☃ image 1]]

equals $\greenD{4}$ otters.

We need to count how many pictures are over each day on the picture graph.  


[[☃ image 1]]

There are $\blueD7$ pictures over Friday.

$\blueD7 \times \greenD4 = \redD{28}$

The tour saw $28$ otters on Friday.

Here is the completed chart:

Day |  Number of otters seen
- | :-: | :-:
Friday|$ 7 \times \greenD{4}=  \redD{28}$
Saturday|$ 3 \times \greenD{4} =  \redD{12}$
Sunday|$ 5 \times \greenD{4} =  \redD{20}$

options.alt: A picture of an otter equals 4 otters.

A picture graph shows the horizontal axis labeled Day and the vertical axis labeled Number of otters seen. Each day is listed along the horizontal axis from left to right as follows: Friday, Saturday, and Sunday. The number of otters seen is represented by the number of pictures of otters plotted on the graph. Friday is represented by 7 pictures of otters, Saturday is represented by 3 pictures of otters, and Sunday is represented by 5 pictures of otters.

assessment_items.question_content:
Buddy Bulldog and all his dog friends love to hide bones! They created a picture graph to show how many bones each of them hid this week.


[[☃ image 1]]


[[☃ image 2]]

**Complete the chart using the picture graph.**

Dog | Number of bones hidden
- | :-: | -
Spot| [[☃ input-number 1]]
Buddy | [[☃ input-number 2]]
Snickers| [[☃ input-number 3]]
Socks| [[☃ input-number 4]]
hints:
Each 

[[☃ image 1]]

equals $\greenD{3}$ bones.

We need to count how many pictures are over each dog on the picture graph.  

[[☃ image 1]]

There are $\blueD4$ pictures over Spot.

$\blueD4 \times \greenD3 = \redD{12}$

Spot hid $12$ bones.

Here is the completed chart:

Dog |  Number of bones hidden
- | :-: | :-:
Spot|$  4\times \greenD{3}= \redD{12}$
Buddy|$6\times \greenD{3}=  \redD{18}$
Snickers|$3 \times \greenD{3}=  \redD{9}\,\,\, $
Socks|$8 \times \greenD{3}=  \redD{24}$

options.alt: A picture of a bone equals 3 bones.

A picture graph shows the horizontal axis labeled Dog and the vertical axis labeled Number of bones hidden. Each Dog is listed along the horizontal axis from left to right as follows: Spot, Buddy, Snickers, and Socks. The number of bones hidden is represented by the number of pictures of bones plotted on the graph. Spot is represented by 4 pictures of bones, Buddy is represented by 6 pictures of bones, Snickers is represented by 3 pictures of bones, and Socks is represented by 8 pictures of bones.

assessment_items.question_content:
Perry Pig asked his friends to vote for their favorite movie.  Then, he made a picture graph to show the results.  

[[☃ image 1]]


[[☃ image 2]]

**Complete the chart using the picture graph.**

Favorite movie | Number of pigs 
- | :-: | -
Charlotte's Web| [[☃ input-number 1]]
Babe | [[☃ input-number 2]]
The Muppet Movie| [[☃ input-number 3]]
hints:
Each 

[[☃ image 1]]

 equals $\greenD{5}$ pigs.

We need to count how many pictures are over each movie on the picture graph.  


[[☃ image 1]]

There are $\blueD4$ pictures over Charlotte's Web.

$\blueD4 \times \greenD5 = \redD{20}$

$20$ pigs voted for Charlotte's Web as their favorite movie.

Here is the completed chart:

Favorite movie | Number of pigs 
- | :-: | :-:
Charlotte's Web|$4 \times \greenD{5}= \redD{20}$
Babe|$7\times \greenD{5}=  \redD{35}$
The Muppet Movie|$5\times \greenD{5} =  \redD{25}$

options.alt: A picture of a pig equals 5 pigs.

A picture graph shows the horizontal axis labeled Movie and the vertical axis labeled Number of pigs. Each Movie is listed along the horizontal axis from left to right as follows: Charlotte’s Web, Babe, and The Muppet Movie. The number of pigs for each movie is represented by the number of pictures of pigs plotted on the graph. Charlotte’s Web is represented by 4 pictures of pigs, Babe is represented by 7 pictures of pigs, and The Muppet Movie is represented by 5 pictures of pigs.

assessment_items.question_content:
Patti Penguin created a chart and a picture graph to show how many of her penguin friends visited her favorite vacation places in the last year. 

Vacation  | Number of penguins 
:- | :-: 
Iceberg Island | $24$ 
Chilly Town | $12$ 
Winter Wonderland | $16$ 


[[☃ image 1]]

 

[[☃ image 2]]



**On the picture graph, each**

[[☃ image 3]]

 **equals [[☃ input-number 1]] penguins.**
hints:
$\greenD{24}$ penguins went to Iceberg Island.  How many pictures are above Iceberg Island on the picture graph?

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD6$ pictures are above Iceberg Island.  

$\blueD6 \times \, \goldD? = \greenD{24}$

$\blueD6 \times \goldD4 = \greenD{24}$

Let's see if $\goldD4$ works for the other vacations.

[[☃ image 1]]

Vacation  || Number of penguins 
:- | :-: | :-: 
Iceberg Island| $\blueD6 \times \goldD4 = \greenD{24}$| $\greenD{24}$ 
Chilly Town |$\gray3 \times \goldD4 = \greenD{12}$| $\greenD{12}$ 
Winter Wonderland| $\purpleC4 \times \goldD4 = \greenD{16}$| $\greenD{16}$

On the picture graph, each

[[☃ image 1]]

 equals $\goldD4$ penguins.

options.alt: A picture of a penguin equals question mark penguins.

A picture graph shows the horizontal axis labeled Vacation and the vertical axis labeled Number of penguins. Each vacation place is listed along the horizontal axis from left to right as follows: Iceberg Island, Chilly Town, and Winter Wonderland. The number of penguins for each vacation place is represented by the number of pictures of penguins plotted on the graph. Iceberg Island is represented by 6 pictures of penguins, Chilly Town is represented by 3 pictures of penguins, and Winter Wonderland is represented by 4 pictures of penguins.
Picture of a penguin.

assessment_items.question_content:
Betty Baker created a chart and a picture graph to show how many cookies she baked in her last $4$ batches. 





Batch  | Number of cookies 
:- | :-: 
Batch $1$ | $32$ 
Batch $2$ | $40$ 
Batch $3$ | $56$ 
Batch $4$ | $48$ 



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]


**On the picture graph, each** 
[[☃ image 3]]**equals [[☃ input-number 2]] cookies.**
hints:
Batch $1$ had $\greenD{32}$ cookies.  How many pictures are above Batch $1$ on the picture graph?


[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD4$ pictures are above Batch $1$.  

$\blueD4 \times \, \maroonD? = \greenD{32}$

$\blueD4 \times \maroonD{8} = \greenD{32}$

Let's see if $\maroonD{8}$ works for the other batches.

[[☃ image 1]]


Batch  || Number of cookies 
:- | :-: | :-: 
Batch $1$| $\blueD4  \times \maroonD8= \greenD{32}$| $\greenD{32}$ 
Batch $2$ |$\gray 5  \times \maroonD8  = \greenD{40}$| $\greenD{40}$ 
Batch $3$| $\purpleC 7  \times \maroonD8 = \greenD{56}$| $\greenD{56}$ 
Batch $4$| $\red6  \times\pink8 = \greenD{48}$| $\greenD{48}$

On the picture graph, each 
[[☃ image 1]]
equals $\maroonD8$ cookies.

options.alt: A picture of a cookie equals question mark cookies.

A picture graph shows the horizontal axis labeled Batch and the vertical axis labeled Number of cookies. Each batch is listed along the horizontal axis from left to right as follows: Batch 1, Batch 2, Batch 3, and Batch 4. The number of cookies in each batch is represented by the number of pictures of cookies plotted on the graph. Batch 1 is represented by 4 pictures of cookies, Batch 2 is represented by 5 pictures of cookies, Batch 3 is represented by 7 pictures of cookies, and Batch 4 is represented by 6 pictures of cookies.
Picture of a cookie.

assessment_items.question_content:
Purdy Pony created a chart and a picture graph to show how many of his pony friends live at each of the ranches in town.





Ranch  | Number of ponies 
:- | :-: 
Horse Haven | $49$ 
Pony Pasture | $28$ 
Equine Lodge | $35$ 



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]


**On the picture graph, each**

[[☃ image 3]]

**equals [[☃ input-number 2]] ponies.**
hints:
Horse Haven has $\greenD{49}$ ponies.  How many pictures are above Horse Haven on the picture graph?

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD7$ pictures are above Horse Haven.  

$\blueD7 \times \,\maroonD? = \greenD{49}$

$\blueD7 \times \maroonD{7} = \greenD{49}$

Let's see if $\maroonD{7}$ works for the other ranches.

[[☃ image 1]]

Ranch  || Number of ponies 
:- | :-: | :-: 
Horse Haven| $\blueD7  \times \maroonD{7} = \greenD{49}$| $\greenD{49}$ 
Pony Pasture |$\gray4  \times \maroonD{7}  = \greenD{28}$| $\greenD{28}$ 
Equine Lodge| $\purpleC5  \times \maroonD{7} = \greenD{35}$| $\greenD{35}$

On the picture graph, each

[[☃ image 1]]

equals $\maroonD{7}$ ponies.

options.alt: A picture of a pony equals question mark ponies.

A picture graph shows the horizontal axis labeled Ranch and the vertical axis labeled Number of ponies. Each ranch is listed along the horizontal axis from left to right as follows: Horse Haven, Pony Pasture, and Equine Lodge. The number of ponies in each ranch is represented by the number of pictures of ponies plotted on the graph. Horse Haven is represented by 7 pictures of ponies, Pony Pasture is represented by 4 pictures of ponies, and Equine Lodge is represented by 5 pictures of ponies.
Picture of a pony.

assessment_items.question_content:
Babs Bunny and her rabbit friends love to collect carrots! They created a chart and a picture graph to show how many carrots each of them collected this week.

Rabbit  | Number of carrots collected 
:- | :-: 
Bugs | $36$ 
Cottontail | $24$ 
Flopsy | $18$ 
Babs | $30$ 

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]


**On the picture graph, each**

[[☃ image 3]]

**equals [[☃ input-number 2]] carrots.**
hints:
Bugs collected $\greenD{36}$ carrots.  How many pictures are above Bugs on the picture graph?

[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD6$ pictures are above Bugs.  

$\blueD6 \times \,\maroonD{?} = \greenD{36}$

$\blueD6 \times \maroonD{6} = \greenD{36}$

Let's see if $\maroonD{6}$ works for the other rabbits.

Rabbit  || Number of carrots collected 
:- | :-: | :-: 
Bugs| $\blueD6  \times \maroonD{6} = \greenD{36}$| $\greenD{36}$ 
Cottontail |$4  \times \maroonD{6} = \greenD{24}$| $\greenD{24}$ 
Flopsy| $3  \times \maroonD{6} = \greenD{18}$| $\greenD{18}$

equals $\maroonD{6}$ carrots.

options.alt: A picture of a carrot equals question mark carrots.

A picture graph shows the horizontal axis labeled Rabbits and the vertical axis labeled Number of carrots collected. Each rabbit is listed along the horizontal axis from left to right as follows: Bugs, Cottontail, Flopsy, and Babs. The number of carrots collected is represented by the number of pictures of carrots plotted on the graph. Bugs is represented by 6 pictures of carrots, Cottontail is represented by 4 pictures of carrots, Flopsy is represented by 3 pictures of carrots, and Babs is represented by 5 pictures of carrots.
Picture of a carrot.

assessment_items.question_content:
Barry Bee and his bee friends love to pollinate flowers! They created a chart and a picture graph to show how many flowers each of them pollinated today. 


Bee  | Number of flowers pollinated   
:- | :-: 
Barry | $27$ 
Bumbly | $63$ 
Betty | $54$ 
Bertie | $45$ 


[[☃ image 1]]

 

[[☃ image 2]]

**On the picture graph, each**

[[☃ image 3]]

**equals [[☃ input-number 2]] flowers.**
hints:
Barry Bee pollinated $\greenD{27}$ flowers.  How many pictures are above Barry on the picture graph?


[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD3$ pictures are above Barry.  

$\blueD3 \times \,\maroonD{?} = \greenD{27}$

$\blueD3 \times \maroonD{9} = \greenD{27}$

Let's see if $\maroonD{9} $ works for the other bees.

[[☃ image 1]]

Bee  || Number of flowers pollinated
:- | :-: | :-: 
Barry| $\blueD3  \times \maroonD{9} = \greenD{27}$| $\greenD{27}$ 
Bumbly |$\gray7 \times \maroonD{9} = \greenD{63}$| $\greenD{63}$   
Betty| $\purpleC6  \times \maroonD{9} = \greenD{54}$| $\greenD{54}$ 
Bertie| $\redC5  \times \maroonD{9} = \greenD{45}$| $\greenD{45}$

On the picture graph, each

[[☃ image 1]]

equals $\maroonD{9} $ flowers.

options.alt: A picture of a flower equals question mark flowers.

A picture graph shows the horizontal axis labeled Bee and the vertical axis labeled Number of flowers pollinated. Each Bee is listed along the horizontal axis from left to right as follows: Barry, Bumbly, Betty, and Bertie. The number of flowers pollinated is represented by the number of pictures of flowers plotted on the graph. Barry is represented by 3 pictures of flowers, Bumbly is represented by 7 pictures of flowers, Betty is represented by 6 pictures of flowers, and Bertie is represented by 5 pictures of flowers.
Picture of a flower.

assessment_items.question_content:
Laura is a zookeeper who loves elephants. All of the zookeepers created a chart and picture graph to show how many elephants each zoo has.

Zoo  | Number of elephants
:- | :-: 
Safari City | $21$ 
Zooville | $28$ 
Animal Land | $35$ 
Tusk Town | $14$ 


[[☃ image 1]]

 

[[☃ image 2]]

**On the picture graph, each**

[[☃ image 3]]

**equals [[☃ input-number 2]] elephants.**
hints:
Safari City has $\greenD{21}$ elephants.  How many pictures are above Safari City on the picture graph?


[[☃ image 1]]

$\blueD3$ pictures are above Safari City.  

$\blueD3 \times \,\maroonD{?} = \greenD{21}$

$\blueD3 \times \maroonD{7} = \greenD{21}$

Let's see if $\maroonD{7} $ works for the other zoos.

[[☃ image 1]]

Zoo  || Number of elephants
:- | :-: | :-: 
Safari City | $\blueD3  \times \maroonD{7} = \greenD{21}$| $\greenD{21}$ 
Zooville |$\greenD4 \times \maroonD{7} = \greenD{28}$| $\greenD{28}$   
Animal Land | $\purpleD5  \times \maroonD{7} = \greenD{35}$| $\greenD{35}$ 
Tusk Town | $\goldE2  \times \maroonD{7} = \greenD{14}$| $\greenD{14}$

On the picture graph, each 

[[☃ image 1]]

 equals $\maroonD{7} $ elephants.

options.alt: A picture of an elephant equals question mark elephants.

A picture graph shows the horizontal axis labeled Zoo and the vertical axis labeled Elephants. Each Zoo is listed along the horizontal axis from left to right as follows: Safari City, Zooville, Animal Land, and Tusk Town. The number of elephants for each zoo is represented by the number of pictures of elephants plotted on the graph. Safari City is represented by 3 pictures of elephants, Zooville is represented by 4 pictures of elephants, Animal Land is represented by 5 pictures of elephants, and Tusk Town is represented by 2 pictures of elephants.
Picture of an elephant.
